Try this: Use the 30min range instead and measure it with the fib tool. You will find an order block of inverse imbalace sitting at the mid point or OTE that starts the main move. Safer entry with tighter stop loss. 10a.m is usually when you get the main move if it's a medium volatile day. Sometime 11a.m is when price pulls back to react off the PDarray at the mid point or OTE. Sometimes when price revisits that range the next day you get another reaction. Lastly, price bounces off the top of the range after 11.30a.m.

The entry at 10:45 was very risky, since it could have easily faked back to the down side, you should wait for at least a 2nd candle to stay above the range

Love the ORB but entry for me is not until it Breaks and Re-Test the High  because fakeouts happen if entered on the break and it can go back into the range all the way to the low. Just happened Monday Presidents Day.

Thanks for this video and the all-important homework. Many times there will be a liquidity grab after breakout, so a variation to this method would be to wait for pullback that respects the OR and then enter on a rejection candle which might increase potential gains.  
Unlike your exhaustive homework, I back tested 23 Nasdaq tickers on Feb Fri 13, without the sell order at bottom and using the pullback/rejection variation and these are the results:  
7 Entries, 9 ORL (low) rejections (that would be your sell orders) and 7 ORH (high) rejections (ie did not respect OR and just took off). 
5 of 7 Entries hit 1.25:1 ratio and same 5 hit 1:1 
Only 2 of 7 ORH rejections  hit 1:25:1 and 1:1  
(For testing I'm using free tier Alpaca IEX so not super accurate) 
From this very limited test it appears that waiting for pullback/rejection gives better gains/less falsies!
Sometimes 5min candle gets you in quicker.
Pre-market rejection of lows can signal immediate entries with better gains.
“ don’t be a dick for a tic” 😂

The word you were looking for is likelihood, not "likeliness."

The 1st trade at 8:22 created a 15 minute order block that then got reversed which is where you took the trade long! Once the 15 closed above that initial drop, that is called a change in state of delivery or Change of Character.
Your trade likely went much higher and if you knew the rules, you could place your stop loss at the top of the candle where the bullish trend started which was the 1st green candle
Would be interesting to test this based on which side of the overnight market opens vs previous day close.

Its a simple strategy, but in reality only is about 50% successful. The big money is trading news and fundamentals not time. They have computer algorithms setup. With all the people trading this strategy it is less effective these days.
Do you wait for candle closure when it breaks the 15?

It is virtually impossible for individuals to day trade for a living, contrary to what course providers claim. We observe all individuals who began to day trade between 2013 and 2015 in the equity futures market, the third in terms of volume in the world. We find that 97% of all individuals who persisted for more than 300 days lost money. Only 1.1% earned more than the minimum wage and only 0.5% earned more than the initial salary of a bank teller — all with great risk.   Chague, Fernando and De-Losso, Rodrigo and Giovannetti, Bruno, Day Trading for a Living? (June 11, 2020).

I don't do 15 mins coz quite often, when NY opens, price just takes off...
You can miss HUGE moves if you wait for 15 mins...
Having said that, i'm not criticizing the 15 min range, coz you also can avoid the fake outs or 'ranging' that sometimes happens....
Essentially, i start with the 5, if nothing presents, i go to 15....
I like your style and this strategy, thank you.
Great explaining ❤
Break and retest

You should really implement volume and retest of the level! The 15 min OR is one of the most manipulated NQ levels.if you volume on the break and waiting for the retest your %i is going to grow a lot!
At least 50% of the time it will go back to 50% or o vwap/ open print and then take off..

 If you begin to apply ta to your orb strategy you will start getting much better results and risk to reward will be greater. 
 Good job though. I can see and tell that you took your time and put a lot of effort into making it as informative as possible. 
 You are also very beautiful by the way.
